findings.summary: Program publish cascades trusted affectedPersonIds without verifying event_people membership, so stale or poisoned version snapshots could notify people outside the active event.
findings.evidence: Added focused regression tests proving both handlers skip affected person ids when the people query returns no event_people-linked rows; targeted vitest suites passed after the join-through-event_people fix.

residual_risks: Automation trigger dispatch still resolves recipients from payload-derived personId and depends on upstream emitters to keep recipient context event-safe.
This audit slice did not run provider-integration or browser checks; it focused on notification/cascade contract and event isolation.

findings.summary: Desktop/tablet navigation exposed the feature-flags admin surface and other privileged entrypoints without role filtering, and the flags page/server actions allowed any authenticated user to read flag state.
findings.evidence: Added regression tests proving Ops users no longer see privileged sidebar links and non-super-admin requests are redirected/rejected from the flags page and flag-read server actions; targeted vitest suites passed after the guard/filter fix.

tests_run: npx vitest run src/components/sidebar.test.tsx -> pass (15 tests)
npx vitest run src/lib/actions/flags.test.ts -> pass (3 tests)
residual_risks: Mobile More menu still uses a separate role map, so sidebar and mobile navigation can drift again unless both surfaces share one navigation policy.
AUD-002 did not finish a full route-by-route audit of dashboard quick actions and every page-level redirect; it closed the confirmed feature-flags/admin-entrypoint leak.

findings.summary: The public registration success page trusted a bare registrationId and rendered it under any event slug, so a copied or guessed registration UUID from Event B could be displayed on Event A's public route.
findings.evidence: Changed the success page to resolve the route event slug first and changed getRegistrationPublic() to query by both eventId and registrationId; added a regression test that proves mismatched-event lookups return 'Registration not found'; focused vitest suite passed.

residual_risks: This slice did not exhaustively audit every public route that accepts opaque IDs or tokens, especially success/confirmation pages outside the registration flow.
The public success page still reveals event-scoped registration metadata to anyone holding the correct registration UUID for that same event; this audit only closed the cross-event mismatch leak.

findings.summary: mergePeople() trusted people-module role checks alone, so an Event Coordinator could merge two people records and repoint registrations, assignments, travel, accommodation, transport, attendance, and certificates for events they were not assigned to.
findings.evidence: Added a focused regression test that models a coordinator assigned to one event while the target people are linked to another; the action now rejects with Forbidden before opening the transaction, and targeted merge/history vitest suites pass.

tests_run: npx vitest run src/lib/actions/person-merge.test.ts -> pass (27 tests)
npx vitest run src/lib/actions/person-history.test.ts -> pass (18 tests)
residual_risks: AUD-004 did not re-audit every other people mutation for per-event write scoping; this fix only closes the confirmed merge path that repointed event-owned records.
Global people rows with no linked event records remain mergeable by coordinators, which may or may not match the intended ownership model for the shared people directory.
